a: Vocab: hello, goodbye, my name is
Sentence frame: I am X years
Practice oral: My name is, I am X

ib.central_idea: We are unique individuals who belong to a community.
lines_of_inquiry: Characteristics that make me unique (names, age, looks)
How I am similar to and different from others
How I belong in my class family
success_criteria: I can say my name, age, and 3 things about me
I can greet a classmate in English
I can point to my self-portrait and describe it
I can listen respectfully to peers

ib.central_idea: Classrooms have objects organized in ways that help us learn.
lines_of_inquiry: Items we find in our classroom
Ways we count and sort them
success_criteria: I can name 10 classroom objects in English
I can sort objects by type or colour
I can say: There are X books

ib.central_idea: Our bodies let us do amazing things, and we must care for them.
lines_of_inquiry: Parts of my body and what each does
How I take care of my body
success_criteria: I can use 'can' to describe actions (I can jump)
I can participate in a group performance

a: Oral practice: I am a X
Rehearse intro: I am a X, I can Y

ib.central_idea: Animals live in different places and have different needs; we share the world with them.
lines_of_inquiry: Different farm animals and their sounds
What animals can and cannot do
How animals are similar to / different from humans
success_criteria: I can name 6 farm animals and their sounds
I can say what each animal can do
I can make a mask and perform as an animal
I can respect animals in my talk

a: Practice oral: This is my mum

ib.central_idea: Families are groups of people connected by love and care.
lines_of_inquiry: Members of my family and their roles
success_criteria: I can name 6 family members in English
I can use 'This is my X' to present photos
I can make a family tree
I can express gratitude (I love my X)

a: Song: I like, I don't like
Plate divided: half veg, quarter protein

ib.central_idea: The foods we eat affect how healthy and happy we feel.
lines_of_inquiry: Foods I like and don't like
Which foods help me grow strong
success_criteria: I can name 10 foods in English
I can say: I like/don't like X
I can sort foods into healthy/treats
I can present my healthy plate

ib.central_idea: Each person has a unique identity shaped by appearance, personality, likes, and relationships.
success_criteria: I can describe myself in 5 sentences
I can use 'I like/don't like + ing'
I can make a booklet with 5 pages

ib.transdisciplinary_theme: Where We Are in Place and Time
ib.central_idea: Homes are organized spaces that reflect how we live.
lines_of_inquiry: Rooms in my home and their functions
success_criteria: I can label 8 rooms and 15 furniture items
I can use prepositions (in, on, under)
I can draw and describe a floor plan
I can give an oral tour (40 words)

a: Use: It has, It can, It eats

ib.central_idea: Pets and wild animals have different needs and deserve care.
success_criteria: I can describe an animal with 'has/can/eats'
I can explain pet care needs
I can present orally (3-4 sentences each)
ib.cambridge_link: Starters (animals, has got, can for ability)

a: Dear friend, I live in X

ib.transdisciplinary_theme: Where We Are in Place and Time
ib.central_idea: Our town is made of places that serve different purposes in our community.
lines_of_inquiry: How to give and follow directions
What each place is used for
success_criteria: I can name 10 places in town
I can give directions (go straight, turn)
I can draw a labelled map
I can write a 30-40 word postcard

a: Sentence frame: For lunch I had

ib.central_idea: Healthy habits at every meal help us feel and grow well.
success_criteria: I can keep a 5-day food diary
I can explain healthy vs treat
I can make a wellness poster

a: I like it because it's fun

ib.central_idea: Hobbies help us express who we are and enjoy our time.
success_criteria: I can describe my hobby (4 sentences)
I can explain 2 reasons I like it

a: On Monday I have X, then Y

ib.central_idea: Schools organize time and activities so we can learn different things.
lines_of_inquiry: My school subjects and what I learn
success_criteria: I can tell time (o'clock, half past)

a: Survey 10 peers: what time do you…?
Oral: most classmates wake up at X

ib.central_idea: Our daily routines follow patterns connected to our body clock.
lines_of_inquiry: Things I do at different times
Why we do things at specific times
success_criteria: I can use adverbs of frequency correctly
I can make a bar chart

ib.central_idea: Weather follows patterns that affect our clothes, activities, and emotions.
lines_of_inquiry: How we measure and describe weather
How weather changes what we do
success_criteria: I can describe weather (sunny, cloudy, rainy)
I can calculate how many sunny days

ib.central_idea: Every species has a role in nature, and humans must protect them.
success_criteria: I can design a campaign poster

a: Oral: when I feel X, I…

ib.central_idea: We all feel emotions, and we can learn strategies to handle them.
success_criteria: I can explain causes (I feel X because)
I can present a feelings kit

ib.central_idea: Communities rely on helpers who each contribute a vital role.
success_criteria: I can name 8 community helpers + their tools
I can perform in a short play
I can interview and write 70w report

a: Use relatives: the park where I play

ib.transdisciplinary_theme: Where We Are in Place and Time
ib.central_idea: Our neighbourhoods are organized spaces that meet people's daily needs.
success_criteria: I can build a scaled 3D model
I can use relative clauses (the park where…)
I can give an oral tour (4 min)

a: Past simple + future: we will see

ib.central_idea: Video lets us document and share our experiences with others.
lines_of_inquiry: Spaces in my school and what happens there
success_criteria: I can film and edit a 2-min video

ib.central_idea: Peruvian biodiversity is our heritage and responsibility to protect.
success_criteria: I can write 80w appeal with modals
I can design a persuasive poster
I can deliver an oral pitch

ib.central_idea: Sports connect cultures around the world and reveal shared values.
success_criteria: I can describe a sport from another country
I can perform at Olympics Day

ib.transdisciplinary_theme: Where We Are in Place and Time
ib.central_idea: Expeditions combine planning, curiosity, and resilience to explore new places.
lines_of_inquiry: What we need for a trip
success_criteria: I can plan a multi-stop route
I can list essential equipment with reasons

ib.central_idea: Who I become is shaped by what I do today.
lines_of_inquiry: What I'm good at and enjoy
success_criteria: I can list 10 jobs and their skills
I can use 'will' for future plans
I can make a vision board

a: Past cont: I was shy when…

ib.central_idea: Our identity is a unique blend of personality, values, and life experiences.
success_criteria: I can produce a mixed-media portrait
I can write a 150w reflective essay

ib.central_idea: Our daily habits and economic choices shape our wellbeing.
lines_of_inquiry: Income, expense, and wants vs needs
success_criteria: I can categorize expenses in a spreadsheet
I can use 'used to' and past continuous
I can write 150w reflection on habits
I can present a budget plan

ib.central_idea: Ecosystems are interconnected, and human actions affect them.
lines_of_inquiry: How we can campaign for change
success_criteria: I can explain 1 Peruvian ecosystem issue
I can use passive voice in writing
I can design a digital leaflet

ib.central_idea: Cultures express identity through traditions, food, and celebrations.
success_criteria: I can represent culture respectfully at fair

ib.central_idea: Wellbeing is a balance of sleep, nutrition, exercise, and mental health.
Stories let us explore human experience and share emotions.
success_criteria: I can adapt to a stage script
I can perform in a group
I can reflect on my performance
ib.cambridge_link: Flyers (narrative, past continuous, tag questions, connectors)

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Relationships shape who we become and how we understand our role in communities.

inquiry_questions.factual: Who are the key people in my life and what roles do they play?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do relationships shape our identity?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is identity determined more by relationships or by individual choice?

assessment_criteria.A: Listening: identify main ideas in conversations about relationships
assessment_criteria.B: Reading: comprehend texts about people and roles
assessment_criteria.C: Speaking: present a relationship map with justification (2-min)

success_criteria: Map 8+ key relationships with clear roles
ib.cambridge_link: KET Writing Parts 6-7 · Speaking collaborative task

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Places carry histories and meaning for the communities that inhabit them.

inquiry_questions.factual: What places make up my neighbourhood?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do places hold meaning for people?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Should neighbourhoods change or preserve their character?

assessment_criteria.A: Listening: understand interviews about local places
assessment_criteria.B: Reading: interpret signs, notices, short articles
assessment_criteria.C: Speaking: narrate a virtual tour (video voiceover)

a: First conditional: if it rains, we'll

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Travel planning balances geography, budget, and cultural curiosity.

inquiry_questions.factual: How do we plan a trip itinerary?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do budget constraints shape travel decisions?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is cultural immersion more valuable than tourism?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Services connect people, and polite communication builds trust.

inquiry_questions.factual: What services exist in our community?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does politeness enable trust in transactions?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can good service exist without real care?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Knowledge of body systems empowers healthy choices.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do systems interact to keep us healthy?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is exercise the most important factor for wellness?

ib.cambridge_link: KET Reading longer articles · Writing informative

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Audio storytelling creates connection with audiences beyond our classroom.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does audio shape storytelling differently from video?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Do we need formal training to become content creators?

assessment_criteria.C: Speaking: record 3-min podcast with clear delivery

ib.cambridge_link: KET Speaking long turn · Writing story

a: Past perfect: before X had happened

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Cultures express identity through music, language, and history — and understanding them broadens our perspective.

inquiry_questions.factual: What cultural elements define a country?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does culture shape individual identity?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can a culture be fully understood by outsiders?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Effective study strategies are shaped by how we learn, not by rules others impose.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does metacognition improve learning?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Are there universal study habits that work for everyone?

success_criteria: Use 2nd conditional (if I studied more, I would…)

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Budget travel is a lens through which we balance exploration with economic reality.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do financial constraints shape travel choices?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is ethical tourism possible for all budgets?

success_criteria: Plan a real trip within budget

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Digital citizenship protects ourselves and others in online spaces.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do our online actions affect others?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Should tech platforms be responsible for user behaviour?

a: Modal passive: the work is done

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Career paths reveal the diverse ways humans contribute to society.

inquiry_questions.factual: What careers exist in our community?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does work shape identity and purpose?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is it better to follow passion or pragmatism in career choice?

ib.cambridge_link: PET bridge (career vocabulary, formal interview register)

ib.statement_of_inquiry: News media shapes public understanding and requires careful, balanced storytelling.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do we balance information vs opinion?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can news ever be objective?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Personality frameworks help us understand ourselves but don't determine who we become.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do frameworks shape self-understanding?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Are personality tests reliable predictors?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Film is a powerful medium that shapes narrative, emotion, and cultural memory.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do films construct meaning?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is film a reflection of culture or a shaper of it?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Health campaigns use evidence and empathy to change behaviours.

inquiry_questions.factual: What are the main health issues in our school?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does data drive health behaviour change?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is awareness enough to change behaviour?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Human rights require protection through critical discussion and civic engagement.

inquiry_questions.factual: What are the UN human rights articles?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How are rights protected or violated?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can universal rights apply across all cultures?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Individual choices aggregate into global environmental impact.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do individual choices connect to global systems?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can personal action alone address climate change?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Literary genres shape how stories reach and affect audiences.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do genre conventions shape meaning?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Do genres limit or enable creative expression?

success_criteria: Adapt a story to a new genre (250w)

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Identity is a dynamic intersection of cultural, social, and personal narratives.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How are identities negotiated within cultures?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is identity primarily individual or collective?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Scientific inquiry reveals patterns through systematic evidence-based methods.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does systematic inquiry reveal patterns?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can science explain everything?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Global challenges require coordinated international responses informed by diverse perspectives.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do nations negotiate shared challenges?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is the UN effective at addressing global problems?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Cultures shape and are shaped by the languages, traditions, and histories they share.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do cultures influence each other?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Does globalization enrich or erode cultures?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: AI raises ethical questions about our future relationship with technology.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do AI tools challenge existing norms?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Should AI development be regulated globally?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Global problems demand creative, systems-level solutions shared widely.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do problems connect across systems?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Whose responsibility is solving global issues?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Personal growth is intentional — we become through reflection, goal-setting, and action.

inquiry_questions.factual: What are my strengths, values, goals?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does metacognition drive growth?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can we engineer our personal growth?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Media literacy is essential for informed citizens in a world of abundant misinformation.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How does media shape public understanding?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Should platforms be required to fact-check?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Research drives progress when questions are well-defined and methods rigorous.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do we design rigorous inquiry?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is rigor more valuable than innovation?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Social movements shape history by mobilizing collective action around shared values.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do movements achieve change?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Are movements more effective than institutions?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Businesses can create value and contribute to sustainability through design choices.

inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do businesses balance profit and planet?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Can capitalism be sustainable?

ib.statement_of_inquiry: Professional identity is crafted through how we present our skills and story.

inquiry_questions.factual: What goes into a CV and cover letter?
inquiry_questions.conceptual: How do professionals signal value?
inquiry_questions.debatable: Is authenticity possible in professional contexts?

ib.essential_understanding: Rhetoric shapes public discourse and reveals how power operates through language.
tok_links: How does rhetoric shape what we know?
Are emotional and logical appeals equally valid?

tok_links: How do theoretical frameworks shape interpretation?
Is the author's intent relevant to interpretation?

ib.essential_understanding: Academic research is a structured conversation that extends human understanding.
tok_links: How do we know what we know?

ib.essential_understanding: Economic policy choices shape inequality, opportunity, and wellbeing at scale.
tok_links: Who benefits and who bears costs?

ib.essential_understanding: Policy briefs translate research into decision-ready recommendations for change.
tok_links: How do we translate evidence into policy?
Whose voices are heard in policy?
success_criteria: Research a Peruvian issue with data

ib.essential_understanding: Ethical reasoning frameworks help us navigate moral dilemmas thoughtfully.
tok_links: How do we decide between competing values?
Are ethics universal or culturally bound?
